title: 11 Herbal Essential Oils For Body Odor
intro_desc: Herbal essential oils have been used for centuries to combat body odor, and it's not just a myth. These oils are distilled from plants and have natural deodorizing properties that can help keep you fresh. For example, Melaleuca alternifolia, also known as tea tree oil, is known for its antimicrobial properties, which help kill bacteria that cause body odor. Cymbopogon citratus, or lemon grass oil, has a refreshing scent that can help mask unpleasant odors, while Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender oil, has a calming effect that can help reduce sweat production.  When we drink herbal teas made from these plants, the compounds are absorbed into our bloodstream and can help combat body odor from the inside out. This is because the plants contain antioxidants and other compounds that help neutralize the bacteria that cause body odor. As a result, you may find that you sweat less and feel fresher for longer. Using herbal essential oils and teas can also have other benefits, such as reducing stress and anxiety, improving sleep, and boosting your immune system. By incorporating these oils and teas into your daily routine, you may find that you feel more confident and comfortable in your own skin.
intro_image_alt: herbal essential oils for body odor

remedies.remedy_desc: Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and linalool as its bioactive constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial properties, which help reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin that can cause body odor. Cineole, in particular, has been found to inhibit the growth of bacteria like Staphylococcus aureus and Escherichia coli, which are common causes of body odor. The antifungal properties of limonene and linalool also help control yeast growth on the skin, another factor that contributes to body odor. By reducing bacterial and fungal growth, Melaleuca alternifolia essential oils can help keep the skin healthy and fresh, reducing the occurrence of body odor.
remedies.image_alt: Melaleuca alternifolia herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Melaleuca alternifolia leaves. Wash them with 1 cup of water to remove dirt.
Add 1 cup of water to a saucepan and bring to boil. Reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes.
Add the washed Melaleuca alternifolia leaves to the saucepan. Simmer for another 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a bowl. Discard the leaves.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like coconut or jojoba oil) to the mixture. Stir well and let it cool. Your Melaleuca alternifolia essential oil is now ready for use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon citratus essential oils contains citral, geraniol, and limonene as its main bioactive constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce sweat and eliminate bacteria that cause body odor. The antimicrobial action of citral and geraniol prevents the growth of bacteria that break down sweat, resulting in unpleasant odors. The anti-inflammatory properties of limonene help to soothe skin irritations and reduce the appearance of body odor. By inhibiting the growth of bacteria and reducing skin irritations, Cymbopogon citratus essential oils helps to prevent body odor and promote a fresher, cleaner feeling.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon citratus herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Cymbopogon citratus leaves and 1 cup of water.
Chop the Cymbopogon citratus leaves into small pieces.
Combine the chopped leaves and water in a saucepan, heat it over medium heat for 10 minutes.
Remove from heat, let it steep for 2 hours. Then, strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Transfer the liquid to a clean glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place. Use 5-7 drops of the essential oil as needed to help control body odor.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia essential oils contains the bioactive constituents linalool and linalyl acetate, which have antimicrobial and antifungal properties. These properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria and fungi on the skin, which can contribute to body odor. The antiseptic properties of linalool and linalyl acetate also help to kill bacteria that can cause bad smells. Additionally, the astringent properties of these constituents help to dry out the skin, reducing sweat and the bacteria that thrive in it. By reducing the amount of sweat and bacteria on the skin, Lavandula angustifolia essential oils can help to prevent body odor.
remedies.image_alt: Lavandula angustifolia herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Lavandula angustifolia flowers. Dry them in a warm, dark place for 2-3 weeks to reduce moisture content.
Add 2 cups of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil) to a saucepan. Heat it on low heat (around 100°F to 150°F) until it's warm.
Add 1/4 cup of dried Lavandula angustifolia flowers to the warm carrier oil. Let it steep for 2-3 hours. Stir occasionally.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle. Discard the solids.
Store the Lavandula angustifolia essential oil in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid. Use 5-7 drops for body odor prevention.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus essential oils contains compounds like cineole, limonene, and alpha-pinene, which have antimicrobial properties. These compounds help reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can cause body odor. Eucalyptus globulus essential oils also has a deodorizing effect due to its ability to inhibit the production of volatile organic compounds (VOCs) that contribute to body odor. The antimicrobial and deodorizing properties of eucalyptus globulus essential oils can help control body odor and leave the skin smelling fresh. The decongestant properties of eucalyptus globulus essential oils also help reduce sweat buildup, which can contribute to body odor.
remedies.image_alt: Eucalyptus globulus herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Eucalyptus globulus leaves and 2 cups of water in a pot.
Boil the water and add the leaves. Let it simmer for 2 hours.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a separate container.
Transfer the liquid to a distillation apparatus or a steam distiller and heat it to separate the essential oil.
Collect the essential oil and store it in a dark glass bottle with a tight-fitting lid.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils contains 1,8-cineole, camphor, and borneol as its major bioactive constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial properties that help reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which can cause body odor. The antiseptic properties of camphor and borneol also help to kill existing bacteria, minimizing the unpleasant smells they produce. The antimicrobial and antiseptic properties of Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils work together to create an environment that is less conducive to the growth and survival of odor-causing bacteria. This makes Rosmarinus officinalis essential oils a potentially effective natural solution for managing body odor.
remedies.image_alt: Rosmarinus officinalis herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Get 1 cup of fresh Rosmarinus officinalis (Rosemary) leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat for 10 minutes, then add the Rosemary leaves.
Let the mixture steep for 1 hour, then strain the liquid using a cheesecloth or a coffee filter.
Add 1 tablespoon of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) to the strained liquid and mix well.
Store the Rosemary essential oil in a dark glass bottle and use 5-7 drops for body odor prevention.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cymbopogon nardus essential oils contains a compound called Citronellal, which is a natural antiseptic and antibacterial agent. This helps to reduce the growth of bacteria on the skin, which is often the cause of body odor. Another key component, Geraniol, has a deodorizing effect and helps to eliminate unpleasant smells. The antifungal properties of Cymbopogon nardus essential oils also prevent the growth of fungi that can contribute to body odor. By controlling the growth of bacteria, fungi, and other microorganisms, Cymbopogon nardus essential oils helps to keep the skin fresh and odor-free.
remedies.image_alt: Cymbopogon nardus herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Cymbopogon nardus leaves, a distillation apparatus, and a carrier oil (like jojoba oil).
Wash the leaves in cold water, and dry them completely.
Place the leaves in the distillation apparatus, and add 2 cups of water.
Heat the water to 212°F (100°C), and collect the steam. The steam will contain the essential oil.
Separate the essential oil from the water using a condenser. Mix the essential oil with the carrier oil (1:1 ratio).

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris essential oils contains thymol and carvacrol as its active constituents. These compounds have antimicrobial and antifungal properties, which help to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi that cause body odor. Thymol is a natural disinfectant that kills odor-causing bacteria, while carvacrol has a deodorizing effect by reducing the production of volatile compounds that give off unpleasant smells. The antiseptic properties of these compounds also help to soothe and balance the skin's natural pH, creating an environment that is less conducive to bacterial growth. As a result, using Thymus vulgaris essential oils may help to reduce body odor and leave the skin feeling fresh and clean.
remedies.image_alt: Thymus vulgaris herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of fresh Thymus vulgaris leaves, a clean glass jar, and a carrier oil like jojoba oil.
Wash the leaves with cold water and pat them dry with a paper towel.
Put 2 tablespoons of the dried leaves in the glass jar and pour 1 cup of the carrier oil over them.
Seal the jar and let it sit in a cool, dark place for 2 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
After 2 weeks, strain the oil through a cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids. Use the oil as needed for body odor.

remedies.remedy_desc: Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains a compound called anethole, which is a natural antifungal and antibacterial agent. This helps to control the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin, reducing sweat odor. The oil also contains limonene, which has deodorizing properties that neutralize unpleasant smells. Additionally, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils contains beta-pinene, a compound that helps to reduce sweat production and prevent bacterial growth. By controlling bacterial growth and reducing sweat, Foeniculum vulgare essential oils can help to minimize body odor.
remedies.image_alt: Foeniculum vulgare herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Foeniculum vulgare seeds and 2 cups of carrier oil (like coconut or olive oil) in a clean glass bottle.
Fill a small saucepan with 4 cups of water and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and add Foeniculum vulgare seeds to the boiling water. Let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into the glass bottle with the carrier oil. Discard the seeds.
Let the mixture sit for 2-3 days, then strain it again through cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle. This is your Foeniculum vulgare essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Santalum album essential oils contains a compound called Santalol, which has antimicrobial properties. This means it helps kill bacteria on the skin, reducing the cause of body odor. Another compound, Beta Santalol, has deodorizing properties, which help neutralize unpleasant smells. The oil's astringent properties also help tighten pores, reducing sweat production, which contributes to body odor. The combination of these properties in Santalum album essential oils makes it a helpful tool in managing body odor.
remedies.image_alt: Santalum album herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 1 cup of Santalum album leaves, 1 cup of water, and a glass jar.
Chop the leaves into small pieces and add them to the glass jar.
Pour the cup of water over the leaves in the jar and stir well.
Let the mixture sit in a warm place for 2-3 weeks, shaking the jar every day.
Strain the liquid and discard the leaves. Your essential oil is now ready to use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale essential oils contains a compound called gingerol, which has antimicrobial properties that help reduce bacteria growth on the skin. This growth is often the cause of body odor. Gingerol also has antifungal properties, which help combat fungi growth, another contributor to body odor. The sesquiterpenes in Zingiber officinale essential oils, specifically zingiberene and bisabolol, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the skin and reduce sweat. By reducing bacteria, fungi, and inflammation, Zingiber officinale essential oils can help minimize body odor.
remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Zingiber officinale roots, a cup of carrier oil (coconut or jojoba oil), and a glass jar for storage.
Wash the roots with 1 cup of water, then dry them completely with a towel.
Place the dried roots in a heat-resistant bowl and add 1 cup of water. Heat it in a double boiler or a saucepan with water until it reaches 180°F (82°C).
Let the mixture steep for 2 hours. Then, strain it through a cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Mix the strained liquid with the carrier oil in a ratio of 1:10. Store the mixture in the glass jar and use it as a body odor essential oil.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita essential oils contains active constituents like limonene, beta-pinene, and linalool. These compounds have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, helping to reduce the growth of odor-causing bacteria on the skin. The deodorizing properties of linalool, in particular, make it effective in neutralizing unpleasant odors. The antimicrobial properties of limonene and beta-pinene also contribute to the reduction of bacteria that can cause body odor. By controlling the growth of bacteria and neutralizing odors, Mentha x piperita essential oils can help to freshen and deodorize the body.
remedies.image_alt: Mentha x piperita herbal essential oils for body odor
remedy_procedure: Gather 2 cups of fresh Mentha x piperita leaves and 1 cup of water in a saucepan.
Heat the water over low heat, then add the Mentha x piperita leaves and let it simmer for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
Transfer the liquid to a glass container and let it sit at room temperature for 2-3 days to allow the essential oil to separate.
Strain the liquid again and transfer the essential oil to a dark glass bottle, using 1 tablespoon of the oil for body odor prevention.

faq_prevent: Drinking herbal tea may help reduce body odor. Some herbal teas, like peppermint and chamomile, have antimicrobial properties that can help kill bacteria that cause sweat to smell. These teas can help keep you fresh and clean, but they won't replace regular showering or hygiene habits. They can be a helpful addition to your daily routine.
faq_safe: Herbal teas can help with body odor, but drinking them daily isn't completely safe. Some herbs, like sage and tea tree, can be too potent and cause stomach problems or interact with medications. Also, relying on tea for odor control might not address the root cause, so it's essential to maintain good hygiene and a balanced diet.
faq_timeline: Herbal teas can help with body odor, but it takes some time to notice results. You might start to feel fresher after a week or two of drinking them regularly. It's because these teas help balance your body's natural chemistry, which can take a few weeks to adjust.
faq_time: Drinking herbal tea can help reduce body odor. If you want to experience its benefits, it's best to have it in the morning. This is because your body has a natural detox process that occurs at this time, making it easier for the tea's ingredients to work effectively and help eliminate toxins that can cause body odor.
faq_interaction: Some herbal teas can interact with prescription medications. If you take medication for body odor, drinking certain herbal teas like peppermint or chamomile might affect how the medication works. This could make the body odor worse or reduce its effectiveness. It's essential to check the ingredients of the herbal tea you're consuming to avoid any potential interactions.
faq_pregnant: When pregnant, it's a good idea to be cautious with herbal teas. Some herbal teas for body odor contain ingredients like tea tree oil or peppermint, which can be safe in small amounts. However, if consumed in large quantities, they might cause issues like stomach cramps or allergic reactions. It's best to drink these teas in moderation.
faq_breastfeeding: If you're breastfeeding and concerned about body odor, herbal teas might be an option. However, some herbs like sage and chamomile are not recommended as they can pass into breast milk and potentially affect a baby's behavior. Look for teas specifically labeled as safe for breastfeeding women.
title_2: Dealing with body odor at the office? Try these 11 herbal essential oils
